Update data contract to match actual artifact schemas

The v0 data contract was stale — referenced columns (run_id, confidence,
period) and tables (Themes, Benchmarks) that don't exist in the actual
artifacts. Rewritten to document the real schemas: review_dimension_scores,
advisor_dimension_scores (3 methods x 6 dimensions), partner_groups_mock,
reviews_clean, EDA artifacts, and metadata.json.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: tunnel forwarding via XDP_PASS + TC egress passthrough

Two issues prevented transit traffic from being forwarded through
GRE/ESP tunnel interfaces:

1. redirect_capable was set for tunnel interfaces, causing
   bpf_redirect_map to send Ethernet-framed packets into POINTOPOINT
   tunnels that expect raw IP. The tunnel driver encapsulated the
   full buffer including the 14-byte Ethernet header, producing
   corrupted GRE payloads (inner "IP [version 0 != 4]").

   Fix: exclude tunnel interfaces (IFACE_FLAG_TUNNEL) from both
   redirect_capable map and tx_ports devmap. XDP falls back to
   XDP_PASS, letting the kernel strip Ethernet and route through
   the tunnel correctly.

2. TC egress dropped tunnel-encapsulated outer packets. When XDP
   does XDP_PASS for tunnel-routed traffic, the kernel forwards
   through the tunnel driver which prepends outer headers to the
   same skb — preserving ingress_ifindex from the original inner
   packet. TC egress saw non-zero ingress_ifindex with no session
   and dropped the outer packet (TC_ACT_SHOT).

   Fix: allow GRE (proto 47) and ESP (proto 50) outer packets
   through TC egress when ingress_ifindex is set. The inner packet
   was already validated by the XDP pipeline; the outer
   encapsulation is trusted local kernel work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add chez-* wrapper modules: networking, compression, PCRE2, YAML, LevelDB

Thin re-export wrappers mapping chez-* libraries to Gerbil-style :std/* paths:
- (std net request) — HTTP client via chez-https
- (std net httpd) — HTTP server via chez-httpd
- (std net ssl) — TLS/TCP via chez-ssl
- (std compress zlib) — gzip/deflate via chez-zlib
- (std text yaml) — YAML parse/emit via chez-yaml
- (std db leveldb) — key-value store via chez-leveldb
- (std pcre2) — PCRE2 JIT regex via chez-pcre2

27 wrapper tests (yaml: 7, zlib: 4, ssl: 4, request: 5, pcre2: 7)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix _ field stripping: apply to allStreams (final pipeline), not just accumulatedStreams

The previous fix only stripped _-prefixed fields in the real-time
accumulatedStreams path (used for early-exit checking). The actual sort/
filter/display pipeline uses allStreams, rebuilt from raw addon results
after Promise.allSettled — without any stripping.

Sootio's _size (raw bytes) survived into allStreams, causing two bugs:
1. Size display: _size bytes returned as GB (154 MB → ~161 million GB)
2. Sort: Sootio streams floated above Comet series streams because
   sort comparator used _size bytes as GB, making them appear huge

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Native Aqua interface with:
- Tabbed UI: Backup, Restore, Inspect, Devices
- Navigation Services file/folder picker dialogs
- Progress bar with real-time updates from CLI subprocess
- Log panel showing operation output
- .app bundle with Info.plist (RustyBackup.app)

Linked against Carbon.framework + libSystem (26KB GUI binary).
CLI binary bundled inside .app at Contents/MacOS/rusty-backup-ppc.
Requires Mac OS X Tiger 10.4+ with active GUI session.

same articles table with body_source='perigon' for M3/M5 processing.

Found 123 gap counties ready for supplemental coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Absorb gherkin compat modules: 23 new stdlib libraries

Adapted from gherkin/src/compat/ with Gerbil-compatible APIs on
native Chez Scheme. No Gambit dependencies.

New modules:
- std/pregexp: full regex engine (Dorai Sitaram's pregexp, 723 lines)
- std/test: test framework (test-suite, test-case, check)
- std/logger: logging with levels (error/warn/info/debug/verbose)
- std/cli/getopt: CLI argument parsing (options, flags, commands)
- std/misc/thread: Gambit-compatible thread API on Chez threads
- std/misc/process: run-process, run-process/batch
- std/misc/queue: mutable FIFO queue
- std/misc/bytes: bytevector bitwise operations
- std/misc/uuid: UUID v4 generation
- std/misc/repr: repr/prn object printing
- std/misc/completion: async completion tokens
- std/text/base64: base64 encode/decode
- std/text/hex: hex encode/decode
- std/text/utf8: UTF-8 utilities
- std/text/csv: CSV read/write
- std/text/xml: SXML → XML serialization
- std/os/env: getenv/setenv/unsetenv
- std/os/temporaries: temp file creation
- std/os/signal: POSIX signal constants + handlers
- std/os/fdio: file descriptor I/O
- std/srfi/srfi-13: SRFI-13 string operations
- std/srfi/srfi-19: date/time handling
- std/crypto/digest: MD5/SHA via openssl CLI

All 289 tests pass (213 existing + 76 new).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Cued Wrapped year-in-review share card and handle fixes

- Add openWrapped() and renderWrappedCard() for year-in-review visual card
- Card shows: cover art strip, stat grid (top type, genres, avg rating, peak month), top rated items

- Add two-button layout in stats modal: Share Stats + Wrapped (gradient button)
- Fix invite messages to use @handle instead of display name

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: NBA AI system — CV tracking, 14 analytics modules, full test suite

- Computer vision pipeline: YOLOv8x detection + DeepSORT tracking,
  court homography calibration (pixel → court feet), ball Kalman filter,
  team assignment via K-means on jersey HSV, scene-cut recalibration

- Feature pipeline (14 modules): spacing, defensive pressure, pick-and-roll,
  off-ball events (cut/screen/drift), passing network, momentum, drive analysis,
  shot creation, space control, play recognition, defensive scheme, game flow,
  micro timing, lineup synergy — all thresholds calibrated to NBA ft/s units

- ML models: win probability, player impact (EPA/100), lineup optimizer,
  momentum detector, shot probability

- REST API (FastAPI), Streamlit dashboard, Flask game browser,

commit.message:
v0.2.0-ppc: Full CLI with backup, restore, inspect, list-devices, rip

Complete reimplementation of rusty-backup CLI in C99 for Mac OS X Tiger PPC:
- MBR partition detection with EBR chain following for logical drives
- APM (Apple Partition Map) detection with driver/free space filtering
- Superfloppy detection (FAT VBR, HFS/HFS+ at offset 1024)
- Backup with per-partition raw imaging and progress bar
- Restore with MBR reconstruction and LBA-correct partition placement
- Inspect with full metadata display (alignment, partition types, sizes)
- Device enumeration via rdisk ioctl (works without root on Tiger)
- Alignment detection: DOS Traditional (255x63), Modern 1MB, Custom GCD
- metadata.json compatible with rusty-backup's format

Tested on Dual G4 Mac running Tiger 10.4.12:
- MBR+FAT16 backup/restore: bit-perfect (MD5 verified)
- APM+HFS backup/restore: bit-perfect (MD5 verified)
- EBR chain: correctly follows extended partitions (2 logical drives)
- list-devices: enumerates 4 disks with accurate sizes via rdisk ioctl

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Strip internal _ fields from raw addon streams before spreading

Sootio (and potentially other addons) return _-prefixed fields like
_size (raw bytes) and _hash that collide with our internal namespace.
The _size bytes value was short-circuiting extractSizeGb, causing 154 MB
files to display as ~161 million GB.

Strip any _-prefixed keys from addon payloads before merging, then
re-attach our own _addonName and _trustProxies fields.

commit.message:
Integrate comic script writing workflow skill across app

Phase-aware AI system: adds writing_phase to issues with 7 cognitive
modes (ideation→art_prompts). Each phase shapes AI behavior via
PHASE_INSTRUCTIONS in the system prompt — what to focus on, what to
avoid, and phase-specific tool priorities. Phase selector in StatusBar
persists to DB.

Smart pacing analysis: updates thresholds to match craft standards
(210 words/page, 35 words/balloon, 9-panel grid valid). Adds balloon
word count tracking, panel economy labels (SPLASH/OPEN/STANDARD/DENSE/
GRID/CRAMPED), and panel count monotony detection.

Page architecture: adds page intention field (setup/reveal/transition/
climax/breathing_room/silent_beat) with warnings when reveals land on
left pages. Panel transition types (McCloud's 6 categories).

Art prompts: new art_prompts table and generate_art_prompt AI tool for
translating panel descriptions into visual briefs with lighting, color,
camera, and mood.

Weave view: scene unit size labels, plotline gap warnings (>6 pages
dark), and intention badges on page cards.

commit.message:
fix(watch): fix off-by-one in file:// URL prefix stripping (#27970)

## Summary

- Fix off-by-one error when stripping `file://` prefix in
`node_fs_watcher.zig` and `node_fs_stat_watcher.zig`
- `"file://"` is 7 characters, but `slice[6..]` was used instead of
`slice[7..]`, retaining the second `/`
- Use `slice["file://".len..]` for clarity, matching the existing
pattern in `VirtualMachine.zig:1750`

The bug was masked by downstream path normalization in
`joinAbsStringBufZ` which collapses the duplicate leading slash (e.g.
`//tmp/foo` → `/tmp/foo`).

## Test plan

- [x] Existing `fs.watch` URL tests pass
(`test/js/node/watch/fs.watch.test.ts`)
- [x] Existing `fs.watchFile` URL tests pass
(`test/js/node/watch/fs.watchFile.test.ts`)
- [x] Debug build compiles successfully

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Bot <claude-bot@bun.sh>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
